campbell.title: Campbell City Council — March 24, 2026
campbell.summary: Campbell City Council held a meeting on March 24, 2026, where they honored a beloved police dog that recently passed away and recognized American Red Cross Month and Disability Awareness Month. The council approved an updated beekeeping rule, discussed a state law about transit-oriented development near transit stations, and reviewed a study about potentially establishing homeless services in the West Valley region.
keyTopics: K9 Lucas memorial - police department's first working dog
Updated beekeeping ordinance approved
State Senate Bill 79 discussion on transit-oriented development
West Valley Homeless Services Feasibility Study and regional task force proposal
City's 2025 financial report accepted

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 31, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council held a special meeting to review and approve work plans for five different city commissions that handle everything from parks and libraries to traffic safety and planning. Each commission presented their priorities and goals for the next fiscal year. The council also briefly met in private to discuss a legal matter.
keyTopics: Parks and recreation priorities for 2026-27
Library and public art commission plans
Traffic safety initiatives
Heritage preservation projects
Planning commission work priorities

los-altos.title: Los Altos City Council — March 24, 2026
los-altos.summary: The Los Altos City Council met on March 24, 2026, to approve routine administrative items including financial reports and meeting minutes, adopt policies on council procedures and affordable housing unit transfers, and plan upcoming community events. The council also received a presentation from the regional transit authority (VTA) and discussed the city's housing development strategy.
keyTopics: Weed abatement program completion for 2026
Affordable housing unit transfer policy changes
Progress Pride and Juneteenth flag raisings approved
Transit authority presentation on regional transportation
Housing Element Implementation Schedule timeline

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — March 17, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on March 17, 2026, to approve routine meeting minutes, review the city's finances from January, and check on progress with housing and general planning goals. The council also approved a new residential development called the Solana Project and finalized plans for community health and senior services.
keyTopics: Solana Project residential development approved and ready to move forward
City finances reviewed for January 2026
Housing Element and General Plan progress evaluated
Community health and senior services work plan set for 2026
Council meeting procedures and minute-taking format updated

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 1, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting on April 1, 2026 to organize upcoming council agendas for April 7 and April 14. Council members reviewed what items should be added to each agenda, set specific times for certain topics to be discussed, and checked whether proper public notice was given for any agenda items.
keyTopics: Finalized the April 7, 2026 council meeting agenda
Organized the April 14, 2026 draft agenda with new items
Set specific discussion times for scheduled agenda items
Verified proper public notice requirements were met
Reviewed public records from March 19-26, 2026

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on March 30, 2026 to approve previous meeting minutes and hear public comments. The council also reviewed reports from various committees and posted agendas for upcoming board and commission meetings.
keyTopics: Approval of previous meeting minutes
Public comment period
Committee and staff reports
Upcoming board and commission meeting schedules
Documents available at City Clerk's Office (500 Castro Street)

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review applications for federal grant money that helps fund affordable housing and community development projects in Sunnyvale. The group approved minutes from their previous meeting and heard presentations from organizations seeking funding to support housing and services for residents.
keyTopics: Federal grant funding for affordable housing projects
Community development block grant applications being reviewed
Human services funding decisions
February meeting minutes approval

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council focused on helping local businesses thrive by launching a license amnesty program, reaching out to shopping centers, and planning summer events to encourage residents to shop locally. They also discussed ways the city can partner with private companies to sponsor events and offer incentives to attract new retail stores and startups to Cupertino.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program - helping businesses get into compliance
Summer 'Shop Local' campaign to boost Cupertino retailers
Shopping center outreach efforts
Public-private partnerships for city event sponsorships
Tax breaks and incentives to attract new retail and startup businesses

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 2, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara held a meeting of its Historical and Landmarks Commission on April 2, 2026, where they reviewed matters related to the city's historic buildings and landmarks. Residents could attend in person or join online via Zoom to watch and participate in the discussion.
keyTopics: Historical and Landmarks Commission hearing
Hybrid meeting format (in-person and Zoom)
Public comment submissions via email by noon on meeting day
Zoom access: Meeting ID 972 3326 2035 or phone 1-669-900-6833

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — March 17, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a routine administrative meeting to handle scheduling and organizational matters. Key actions included approving meeting minutes, planning the calendar for upcoming meetings, and authorizing Mayor Montano to attend the WELL 2026 Annual conference.
keyTopics: Approved City Council meeting minutes and confirmed 2026 meeting calendar
Mayor Montano authorized to travel to WELL 2026 Annual conference
Confirmed appointment of school district representative to Economic Development board
Reviewed upcoming agenda items requested by council members

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026 to handle several routine matters. They recognized March as Women's History Month, approved an agreement with the American Red Cross to use city facilities during emergencies, and authorized a three-year contract with Insight Public Sector for email security software services costing about $93,400 per year.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
Red Cross disaster relief facility agreement
Email security software contract ($93,383/year for 3 years)
Late agenda items added to meeting
